channel.title: OpenEmu-Silicon Changelog
channel.description: Most recent changes with links to updates.

item.title: OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.6
item.description:
OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.6

What's New in 1.0.6

  • Flycast updated to version 2.4

Bug Fixes

  • Loading a save state created on an older core version now shows a warning instead of silently crashing
  • Dreamcast BIOS files (dc_flash.bin) are now correctly recognised in System Files — all regional variants are accepted and duplicate entries are collapsed into a single row
  • Core updates no longer stall or repeatedly offer the same version due to a URLSession cache issue

item.title: OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.5
item.description:
OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.5

What's New in 1.0.5

### GameCube is here

The Dolphin core is now available for download from Preferences → Cores. GameCube games load and run natively on Apple Silicon with full Metal rendering and save state support. Wii support is included but has not been fully tested — treat it as experimental for now.

**Migrating from original OpenEmu?** Dolphin save states from the original app are not compatible due to the x86 → arm64 architecture change and different Dolphin version. You'll see a recovery dialog on first launch — choose "Reopen without loading state" to continue. Your memory card saves are not affected.

### Dreamcast — semi-working

Flycast is available and most games are playable. There is a known cold-boot issue on some titles where the first launch shows a black screen — close the game and relaunch and it will work. Full details and ongoing investigation in [issue #169](https://github.com/nickybmon/OpenEmu-Silicon/issues/169).

### 3DO now available

The 4DO core builds natively on arm64 and is now downloadable from Preferences → Cores. Requires the `panafz10.bin` BIOS file in `~/Library/Application Support/OpenEmu/BIOS/`.

### Coming soon — libretro bridge

We're building a translation layer that will allow libretro cores to run inside OpenEmu without being rewritten from scratch. This opens the door to systems that were previously impossible to support — MAME/Arcade, Commodore 64 (VICE), Nintendo 3DS, and others. The first proof of concept (Game Boy via Gambatte) is working. This will ship in a future release.

Bug Fixes

**GameCube / Dolphin**

  • Fixed crash when autoloading a save state before the Metal renderer is fully initialized
  • Fixed save state completion timing — states now save and restore reliably
  • Fixed audio routing through the OE pull model, resolving volume and buffer issues
  • Fixed Classic Controller trigger and Nunchuk axis mappings
  • Fixed Wii system plugin header paths and missing controller images
  • Replaced placeholder Wii controller image with Wiimote + Nunchuk artwork
  • Added Wii system plugin so Dolphin handles both GameCube and Wii games from the same core

**Dreamcast / Flycast**

  • Fixed cold-launch black screen on ARM64
  • Fixed game loop hang on quit
  • Restored audio back-pressure to prevent 3–5× speed gameplay on warm boot
  • Fixed save state race condition on ARM64
  • Prevented duplicate game windows when relaunching a running game

**Game Boy / Gambatte**

  • Fixed white screen on launch caused by unallocated video buffer

**General**

  • Fixed crash when a system plugin had a nil controller description
  • Fixed keyboard remapping being blocked when HID access status was unknown
  • Fixed Sentry consent dialog hanging the main thread on first launch
  • Fixed a warning that now appears before deleting an incompatible save state (instead of silently deleting)
  • Fixed box art lookup — libretro-thumbnails is now used as a third-tier fallback when OpenVGDB and ScreenScraper return nothing

item.title: OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.4
item.description:
OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.4

What's New in 1.0.4

  • Image Adjustments — Saturation and Gamma sliders now stay open while dragging; no longer dismissed by the drag gesture
  • Preferences window now opens at the correct full width on first launch, instead of starting narrow until the Controls tab is visited
  • Homebrew tap — OpenEmu-Silicon is now installable via `brew install --cask openemu-silicon`
  • Crash reporting — opt-in Sentry crash reporting now includes additional context, breadcrumbs, and release tracking to help diagnose issues faster

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed a crash on macOS 26 caused by mixing `NSLog` format strings with Swift string interpolation
  • Fixed sidebar not refreshing after a ROM import completes
  • Fixed sidebar not updating when a homebrew game was added via Core Data
  • Fixed systems the user explicitly disabled being re-enabled on next launch
  • Fixed a potential crash on quit caused by Core Data writes happening off the correct queue
  • Fixed a memory leak from a dangling `NotificationCenter` observer in the game controls bar
  • Fixed Saturation and Gamma slider positions in the Gameplay preferences pane
  • Fixed the Image Adjustments menu item being grayed out in certain conditions
  • Fixed Gameplay preferences pane being clipped at the bottom on first visit
  • Fixed Help menu links (Submit Feedback, User Guide, Release Notes, Web Site) pointing to the original OpenEmu repo instead of OpenEmu-Silicon

item.title: OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.3
item.description:
OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.3

Critical hotfix: emulation cores failed to load in v1.0.2 due to a release-signing bug.

  • Fixed: All emulation cores failing to load with "couldn't be loaded, try reinstalling" — caused by missing entitlements in the v1.0.2 release build stripping disable-library-validation from the signed app
  • Improved: App now re-signs installed cores on first launch after each update, preventing similar issues if a future build has a signing regression

item.title: OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.2
item.description:
OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.2

Core distribution overhaul, bug fixes, and UX improvements.

  • New: Self-hosted appcasts for all 23 cores — every core now delivers the correct ARM64-native binary and can update independently of the app
  • Fixed: Duplicate cores in Preferences → Cores (Mednafen, Mupen64Plus, Atari800, and others appearing twice)
  • Fixed: "doesn't contain a version for the current architecture" error — Intel-only cores are now detected and replaced automatically on every launch
  • Fixed: Keyboard input not working after granting Input Monitoring permission — no restart needed
  • Fixed: Setup Assistant hang when 4DO or DeSmuME were selected
  • Improved: Sidebar hides systems with no installed core and no games
  • Improved: 4DO and DeSmuME show a greyed "Unavailable" button instead of a broken Install button
  • Improved: Updated app icon

item.title: OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.1
item.description:
OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.1

Patch release fixing a critical crash affecting all emulation cores on some machines.

  • Fixed: All emulation cores crashing immediately with "has quit unexpectedly" on affected machines — caused by missing entitlements on the helper process under macOS hardened runtime
  • Fixed: JIT/dynarec emulation cores (mGBA, PPSSPP, and others) unable to run due to missing allow-jit entitlement
  • Improved: XPC broker handshake timeout increased from 2s to 10s, reducing spurious launch failures on slower Macs

item.title: OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.0
item.description:
OpenEmu-Silicon 1.0.0

First public release — native Apple Silicon port of OpenEmu.

  • All 25+ emulation cores rebuilt for ARM64 — no Rosetta required
  • Sony PSP — PPSSPP-Core fully integrated
  • Nintendo 64 — Mupen64Plus revived
  • Sega Dreamcast — migrated from Reicast to Flycast
  • ScreenScraper cover art — automatic box art fetching from ScreenScraper.eu
  • SNES9x updated to 1.63, mGBA updated to 0.10.5
  • Pokémon Mini and Watara Supervision fixed
  • macOS 26 (Tahoe) compatibility fixes throughout
